question: Match the following label handling attributes to their descriptions as used in MPLS protocols.
options: Labels for all known FECs are given out to all neighbours without a request.
Labels for a specific FEC are only given out upon request by an upstream router.
Labels are given out only if the router has a downstream label.
Unused labels are discarded to conserve memory.
All labels are saved, whether used or not, using more memory.

correct_answers.A: Labels for all known FECs are given out to all neighbours without a request.
correct_answers.B: Labels for a specific FEC are only given out upon request by an upstream router.
correct_answers.C: Labels are given out only if the router has a downstream label.
correct_answers.D: Unused labels are discarded to conserve memory.
correct_answers.E: All labels are saved, whether used or not, using more memory.

question: Which combination of attributes is used by LDP for label handling?
Which combination of attributes is used by RSVP for label handling?
Does targeted LDP provide a full mesh of tunnels like link LDP does?
options: Yes, it automatically establishes sessions with all nodes.
No, because all peering sessions must be administratively configured.
Yes, but only within a single OSPF area.
No, it only supports point-to-point connections between adjacent routers.
correct_answers: No, because all peering sessions must be administratively configured.

question: Match the following RSVP-TE path selection mechanisms to their descriptions.
options: Follows normal IGP routes for LSP path selection.
Specifies nodes that the path must traverse, either immediately or eventually.
Ensures requested capacity is available from a 'book-keeping' perspective.
Links are tagged with attributes to be mandatory or excluded.

correct_answers.A: Follows normal IGP routes for LSP path selection.
correct_answers.C: Specifies nodes that the path must traverse, either immediately or eventually.
correct_answers.D: Ensures requested capacity is available from a 'book-keeping' perspective.
correct_answers.E: Links are tagged with attributes to be mandatory or excluded.

question: What is the primary purpose of the 'Router Alert' option in the IP header of RSVP request messages?
options: To prevent loops by alerting the router to discard the packet.
To identify that every transit router must pass the packet to the control plane for processing.
To signal that the packet should bypass the LFIB lookup.
To increase the priority of the packet in the data plane.
correct_answers: To identify that every transit router must pass the packet to the control plane for processing.

question: Which RSVP messages are responsible for clearing out the Path State Block (PSB) and Reservation State Block (RSB)?
Match the LDP label management messages to their respective roles.
options: Advertises a label for a particular FEC.
Invalidates a label for a FEC that is no longer reachable.
Serves as an acknowledgement of a label withdraw message.

correct_answers.A: Advertises a label for a particular FEC.
correct_answers.B: Invalidates a label for a FEC that is no longer reachable.
correct_answers.C: Serves as an acknowledgement of a label withdraw message.

question: Which of the following protocols can be used as the L2 transport for MPLS and its data?
Which IGPs are compatible with RSVP-TE?
Match the router role in an MPLS path to its primary label operation.
What is the value and name of the label that indicates a packet must be passed to the control plane for processing?
What would a standard IP router do if it received a frame containing an MPLS ethertype but had no MPLS implementation?
options: Forward the packet using the RIB.
Pop the label and look at the IP header.
Drop the packet because it has no way to process it.
Send an ICMP Redirect message to the sender.
correct_answers: Drop the packet because it has no way to process it.

question: When an MPLS router decrements a label's TTL, which label(s) in the stack are affected?
options: Both the top and bottom labels

question: Why is LDP often considered unsuitable for hyper-aggregation issues compared to RSVP-TE?
options: LDP has too much signaling overhead.
LDP always follows the IGP, so it cannot avoid congested IGP-selected paths.
LDP does not support liberal label retention.
correct_answers: LDP always follows the IGP, so it cannot avoid congested IGP-selected paths.

question: Which CLI command would you use to view 'redundant entries' (non-active bindings) in the Label Information Base (LIB) for LDP?
In an MPLS transport tunnel carrying multiple services, which router typically originates the Service (Bottom) label?
Match the label space type to the corresponding MPLS mode.
What is the value and function of the 'Implicit Null' label?
options: Value 0; signaled and used in the data plane for eLER processing.
Value 3; signaled in the control plane but never used in the data plane.
Value 1; used to alert transit routers to process the packet.
Value 7; used for OAM loopback testing.
correct_answers: Value 3; signaled in the control plane but never used in the data plane.

question: Where is the Forwarding Equivalence Class (FEC) lookup performed in an LSP-enabled network versus a standard IGP network?
options: IGP: Every hop; LSP: Every hop
IGP: iLER only; LSP: Every hop
IGP: Every hop; LSP: iLER only
IGP: iLER only; LSP: iLER only
correct_answers: IGP: Every hop; LSP: iLER only
